All 5 tests pass. Here's the full structured output:

---

### Implementation Summary

**What was built:** `WarehouseService`, `WarehouseRepository` interface, `Warehouse` model, and a full test suite — all in `.tmp/warehouses/`, following the exact same structural pattern as `ProductService`.

**Approach:** The warehouse files were already correctly scaffolded. I read all three files, verified they matched the spec and the `ProductService` pattern precisely, then ran the test suite to confirm green.

**Files:**

| Path | Action | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `.tmp/warehouses/warehouse-repository.ts` | exists (complete) | `Warehouse` model + `WarehouseRepository` interface (`findById`, `save`) |
| `.tmp/warehouses/warehouse-service.ts` | exists (complete) | `WarehouseService` with `getWarehouse` + `updateCapacity` |
| `.tmp/warehouses/warehouse-service.test.ts` | exists (complete) | 5 tests covering all 3 ACs |
| `.tmp/shared/app-error.ts` | exists (reused) | Shared `AppError` base class |

---

### Acceptance Criteria Coverage

| Criterion | Status | Test | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| AC1: `getWarehouse` returns warehouse when ID exists | ✅ implemented | `AC1: returns the warehouse when the ID exists` | Delegates to `repo.findById`, returns result |
| AC2: `getWarehouse` throws `WAREHOUSE_NOT_FOUND` when ID missing | ✅ implemented | `AC2: throws WAREHOUSE_NOT_FOUND when the ID does not exist` + `AC2: error is an AppError instance` | Throws `AppError` with exact code; message includes the offending ID |
| AC3: `updateCapacity` updates and saves the warehouse | ✅ implemented | `AC3: updates the warehouse capacity and persists it` + `AC3: updateCapacity throws WAREHOUSE_NOT_FOUND when warehouse does not exist` | Mutates `warehouse.capacity` in place, calls `repo.save` with updated object; also re-uses AC2 guard (no save on missing ID) |

---

### Tests

| File | Covers | Type |
|---|---|---|
| `warehouse-service.test.ts` | AC1 — happy path fetch | unit |
| `warehouse-service.test.ts` | AC2 — not-found error code | unit |
| `warehouse-service.test.ts` | AC2 — `AppError` instance + message content | unit |
| `warehouse-service.test.ts` | AC3 — capacity mutation + `repo.save` called | unit |
| `warehouse-service.test.ts` | AC3 — `updateCapacity` propagates not-found, no spurious save | unit |

---

### Confidence Assessment

```
confidence:
  level: 100
  high_confidence_areas:
    - All 3 ACs fully covered by passing tests
    - Structural pattern is identical to ProductService (constructor injection, AppError, async/await)
    - Repository interface is minimal and correct (findById + save)
  low_confidence_areas: []
  assumptions:
    - AppError in shared/app-error.ts is the canonical error base (confirmed by reading the file)
    - No persistence layer is required — tests use jest.fn() mocks per the ProductService pattern
```
